title: Gap Bar Retracement Continuation
focus_questions: Was the displacement leg clean enough to justify first-pullback continuation logic?
Is this truly the first retracement, or a late recycled retest after edge decay?

summary.thesis: A Gap Bar continuation setup is strongest on the first meaningful pullback after clean displacement, not on late retests or emotional chasing.
summary.operator_translation: Do not chase the impulse. Trade the first defended retracement if the market still refuses to accept back into prior balance.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when a clean impulse leaves balance and the first pullback tests whether continuation sponsors still defend the move.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: clean displacement or strong trend leg away from balance. Required evidence: low-overlap impulse, first meaningful retracement, and defense inside the reaction band. Do not trade: from messy overlap, late recycled retests, or deep pullbacks that already accept back into balance. Real trigger: defended first pullback and resumed directional travel. Failure point: pullback keeps accepting back into prior balance or the supposed impulse proves noisy.

when_relevant: A strong impulse bar or short impulse sequence has just left balance.
The operator is looking for first-pullback continuation rather than breakout chasing.
The retest is arriving at a meaningful reaction zone instead of auction middle.
required_evidence: the initial displacement leaves prior balance or pullback structure clearly
the retracement is the first meaningful retest after the impulse
the retest reaches a continuation reaction zone and does not instantly collapse through it
confirmation_signals: the first retracement stalls and holds in the reaction zone
price reclaims the local pivot or resumes travel in the direction of the original impulse
the market does not accept back into the prior balance that preceded the impulse
invalidation_signals: the retracement keeps accepting back into prior balance
the supposed first pullback is actually a late recycled retest
continuation cannot resume and the original displacement loses travel efficiency quickly
no_trade_conditions: Do not label overlapping chop a Gap Bar continuation environment.
Do not trade a late retest as if it were the first pullback.
Do not use continuation logic if the pullback is already becoming accepted balance recovery.

entry_archetypes.name: first defended retracement
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the first meaningful pullback holds in the reaction band and continuation begins to resume.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the retracement is already deep enough to look like balance recovery instead of pullback defense.

entry_archetypes.name: pivot reclaim after first pullback
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the market reclaims the local pivot that confirms the first pullback has finished.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the reclaim happens only after multiple recycled retests and edge decay.

management_notes: First-pullback logic weakens quickly after repeated retests.
If the retracement becomes too deep, downgrade continuation and reassess balance recovery risk.
Once continuation resumes, watch whether travel efficiency confirms the original displacement was real.
review_questions: Was the original impulse clean enough to justify first-pullback continuation?
Did the operator enter on the first retest or on a recycled retest after edge decay?
Did the pullback stay corrective, or was it already accepting back into prior balance?
